path: zHide/Media & Files/Abbreviations used in Obstetrics and Gynaecology .pdf
text: Commonly use abbreviations for Obstetrics and Gynaecology Lexie Tregonning 2012 Page 1 AC Abdominal circumference AFI Amniotic fluid index AFP Alpha-fetoprotein ANC Antenatal care / clinic APH Antepartum Haemorrhage ARM Artificial rupture of membranes BPD Biparietal diameter CRL Crown-rump length CS Caesarean Section CTG Cardiotocograph CVS Chorionic villus sampling DCDA Dichorionic diamniotic ECV External cephalic version EDD Estimated date of delivery EFM External fetal monitoring EFW Estimated fetal weight ELUSCS Elective lower uterine segment caesarean section ERPC Evacuation of retained products of conception FBS Fetal blood sampling fFN Fetal fibronectin FL Femur length FM Fetal movements FSE Fetal scalp electrode FSH Follicle stimulating hormone G Gravida (number of pregnancies regardless of outcome) GBS Group B streptococcus GDM Gestational diabetes mellitus HC Head circumference HDN Haemolytic disease of the newborn HELLP Haemolysis, elevated liver enzymes and low platelets HIE Hypoxic-ischaemic encephalopathy HPL Human placental lactogen HVS High vaginal swab IOL Induction of labour IUGR Intrauterine growth restriction IVH Intraventricular haemorrhage LH Lutenizing hormone LLETZ Large loop excision of the transformation zone LMP Last menstrual period MVP Maximum vertical pocket MCDA Monochorionic diamniotic MCMA Monochorionic monoamniotic MROP Manual removal of placenta NEC NELUSCS Necrotizing enterocolitis Non-elective lower uterine segment caesarean section NND Neonatal death (born alive and died within 4 wks of birth) NTD Neural tube defect OA Occipito-anterior Commonly use abbreviations for Obstetrics and Gynaecology Lexie Tregonning 2012 Page 2 OP Occipito-posterior OT Occipito-transverse P Para (number of live births after 20 wks) PAPP-A Pregnancy-associated plasma protein PE Pre-eclampsia or pulmonary embolus PET Pre-eclampsia toxaemia PID Pelvic inflammatory disease PIH Pregnancy induced hypertension PPH Postpartum Haemorrhage PNC Postnatal care PROM Prelabour rupture of membranes PPROM Preterm prelabour rupture of membranes PPPROM Prolonged preterm prelabour rupture of membranes RDS Respiratory distress syndrome SB Stillbirth (born after 20 weeks with no fetal heart rate) SFH Symphysis-fundal height SGA Small for gestational age SROM Spontaneous rupture of membranes SVD Spontaneous vaginal delivery TTN Transient tachypnoea of the newborn TTTS Twin to twin transfusion syndrome USS Ultrasound scan VBAC Vaginal birth after caesarean VE Vaginal examination
